subject: Q1 Sugar Order — Siam Sugar Partners
body:
Hi Wichai,

Happy new year. We'd like to place our Q1 order — 45 MT raw cane sugar ICUMSA 150. Please confirm pricing per the contract and send a proposed delivery schedule.

David

subject: RE: Q1 Sugar Order — Siam Sugar Partners
body:
Dear David,

Thank you for order, happy new year also. We can confirm 45 MT ICUMSA 150 for Q1. Price is $398 per MT as per the contract agreement.

For delivery, we propose the vessel depart Bangkok port around January 25, arrive your port approximately February 15. Please kindly advise if this schedule is acceptable for you.

Best regards,
Wichai

subject: RE: Q1 Sugar Order
body:
Wichai,

Schedule works. Please proceed and send the pro-forma invoice.

David

subject: Q1 shipment dispatch
body:
Dear David,

Please be inform that the Q1 shipment of 45 MT sugar has been load and dispatch from Bangkok port on January 28. The vessel name is Thai Star 7, expected arrive at your port on February 18-20.

The bill of lading we will send you by email tomorrow.

Thank you,
Wichai

subject: Shipment received — Q1
body:
Wichai,

Shipment arrived and passed quality inspection — ICUMSA 150 confirmed. Invoice received, payment being processed.

David

subject: Q2 pricing proposal
body:
Dear David,

Good day. My name is Nareenart Prasit, commercial manager at Siam Sugar. I am contact you regarding Q2 pricing.

As you are know, the ICE No.11 benchmark has move upward since we sign the contract. For Q2 we propose the price of $420 per unit, this is reflect the current market condition.

Please kindly advise if this price is acceptable for your planning purpose. We look forward to continue good business relation.

Best regards,
Nareenart Prasit
Commercial Manager, Siam Sugar Partners

subject: RE: Q2 pricing proposal
body:
Nareenart,

Thanks for the note. Can you clarify — when you say $420 per unit, what is the unit here? Our contract is priced per metric ton (MT). Is this $420/MT?

Also, our contract requires 21 days written notice for any price change request. What date are you treating as the official notice date for this proposal?

David

subject: RE: Q2 pricing proposal — clarification
body:
Dear David,

Thank you for question. The price is $420/MT — this is same unit as in contract. We treat our email of March 3 as the official notice. So Q2 delivery starting April, the new price will apply.

We are happy to discuss if you have concern.

Nareenart

subject: RE: Q2 pricing — our position
body:
Nareenart,

Thank you for the clarification on the unit. On the price increase — we've reviewed against the contract terms. The ICE benchmark does support some movement, but $420/MT vs $398/MT is a 5.5% increase. We'd counter at $408/MT.

On notice: March 3 gives 21 days to March 24, so Q2 deliveries starting after March 24 could potentially be affected if we agree. We have not yet agreed.

Can we get on a call this week to resolve?

David

subject: Q2 order — please confirm
body:
Dear David,

We have not yet receive the Q2 purchase order. Please kindly advise the volume and whether the pricing discussion with Nareenart is conclude so we can proceed the preparation.

We need the PO by April 14 for the vessel booking.

Thank you,
Wichai

subject: Q2 order — 45 MT, pricing TBD
body:
Wichai,

Please proceed with 45 MT Q2 order at the contract rate of $398/MT for now. We are still working through the pricing discussion with Nareenart and have not reached agreement on the $420/MT proposal. Any agreed change will be reflected in an amendment.

David

subject: Pricing — still outstanding
body:
Dear David,

The Q2 shipment has deliver but the invoice still show $398/MT. We send the notice in March and have not receive written agreement to stay at old price. Please advise when this will resolve.

Nareenart

subject: RE: Pricing — still outstanding
body:
Nareenart,

I understand your position. Here is ours: we have not agreed to $420/MT. We countered at $408/MT on March 18 and never received a response to that counter. If you are willing to meet at $408/MT, I can get this processed. If not, we remain at contract price of $398/MT until a written amendment is signed by both parties.

Please confirm your position so we can close this out.

David

subject: RE: Pricing — response
body:
Dear David,

Thank you for the clear position. We cannot accept $408/MT, our cost do not allow. We need minimum $415/MT to make this work. This is our final offer.

We hope to resolve quickly as Q3 planning will also need new price to be confirm.

Nareenart

subject: Q3 planning — volume needed
body:
Dear David,

I hope you are well. I am writing about Q3 planning — we need to reserve vessel space by June 20 for July-August delivery window. Can you advise what volume you expect for Q3?

Also I understand Nareenart is still discuss pricing with you. I hope this can resolve soon as it affect our planning also.

Thank you,
Wichai

subject: RE: Q3 planning + pricing update
body:
Wichai, Nareenart,

For Q3 we plan 45 MT again. On pricing — I've taken Nareenart's $415/MT counter to my manager. We can move to $412/MT as a final position. This is our ceiling. If we cannot agree, Q3 will proceed at contract price of $398/MT and we'll escalate the pricing dispute to a formal review under the contract mechanism.

I need a response by June 18 to make your vessel booking window.

David
